content.question: Do Germany's partial detracking reforms (two-tier consolidation) offer a politically and institutionally feasible alternative to full abolition of Hungary's tracks, and would partial reform achieve measurable equity gains?
content.reduction_path: Model compressed two-tier tracking alternatives for Hungary (e.g., merging 4-year and 6-year tracks while preserving 8-year gimnazium), and conduct small-scale pilot or simulation of outcomes using Hungarian PISA/panel data with alternative track configurations
